Add \\uhhhh unicode escape support to parser (#142)

Parse \\uhhhh (exactly 4 hex digits) in regex patterns, matching JDK
behavior. When a high surrogate is followed by a \\uhhhh low surrogate,
they are combined into a single supplementary code point.

Fixes #133

fix(#27): set contentWindowInsets=0 on Scaffold to fix IME gap on Android 12+

On API 30+ with enableEdgeToEdge(), adjustResize is ignored. Scaffold's default
contentWindowInsets consumes the bottom inset before imePadding() can act on it.
Setting WindowInsets(0) lets imePadding() receive the full IME inset correctly.
Also restore navigationBarsPadding() on InputBar and add outlineVariant border
to fenced code blocks for clearer visual separation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

      update was rolled back or never fully applied.

commit.message:
Replace thermomachine dynamic per-tick power with flat target-based draw (#617)

Multiple thermomachines on the same gas network race on the shared area
power total: each tick they remove their old registration and add a new
one, causing compounding interference when two machines process in the
same tick.

## Changes

- **New `recalculate_power()` proc**: computes `active_power_usage` from
the target temperature offset from the current gas temp, registered once
via standard `set_active_power()`/`set_idle_power()`:
  ```dm
  var/temperature_difference = abs(target_temperature - old_temperature)
active_power_usage = max(idle_power_usage, (heat_capacity *
temperature_difference) / 1000)
  ```
- **Removed**: `last_registered_power` variable and the entire per-tick
`addStaticPower`/`removeStaticPower` dance from `process_atmos()`
- **`recalculate_power()` called on all state-changing paths**:
`RefreshParts()`, `ui_act()` (power toggle + target change),
`CtrlClick()`, `freezer/on/Initialize()`, `coldroom/Initialize()`,
`freezer/AltClick()`, `heater/AltClick()`
- **Restored conditional `update_parents()`** in `process_atmos()` —
only fires when temperature actually changed by >1K, matching the
`heat_exchanger` pattern
- **Removed duplicate `investigate_log`** in `CtrlClick()` that was
referencing the wrong `usr` variable

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> ## Problem
> 
> Thermomachines currently attempt to dynamically calculate power usage
based on the temperature delta of gas passing through them each tick.
This causes issues when multiple thermomachines are connected to the
same gas network:
> 
> 1. Each machine removes its old power registration and adds a new one
every tick
> 2. When two machines process in the same tick, they interfere with
each other's calculations
> 3. The area power system uses a single total value per channel, not
per-machine tracking
> 4. This causes unpredictable power draws that can compound incorrectly
> 
> The original fix (marked with `// PENTEST FIX - THERMOMACHINE POWER
CALCULATION`) tried to address negative power by tracking
`last_registered_power`, but the fundamental issue is that multiple
machines modifying the same area power total in the same tick causes
race conditions.
> 
> ## Solution
> 
> Replace the dynamic per-tick power calculation with a flat power draw
based on the target temperature difference from room temperature
(similar to how most other machinery works):
> 
> 1. Calculate power draw based on how far the target temperature is
from room temperature (T20C)
> 2. Use this as the machine's active_power_usage (static, doesn't
change every tick)
> 3. Only recalculate when the target temperature changes or machine
turns on/off
> 4. Add a safety check to ensure power draw can never go negative
> 5. Remove the dynamic per-tick power registration system
> 
> This approach:
> - Makes power draw predictable and stable
> - Prevents machines from interfering with each other
> - Follows the pattern used by other machinery in the codebase
> - Lore explanation: "Thermomachines run at full power based on their
target setting because the heating/cooling elements and compressors
operate at constant draw, even if the pipes are well-insulated"
> 
> ## Implementation Details
> 
> Modify
`code/modules/atmospherics/machinery/components/unary_devices/thermomachine.dm`:
> 
> 1. Remove the dynamic power calculation from `process_atmos()`
> 2. Remove the `last_registered_power` variable and all references to
it
> 3. Add a new proc `recalculate_power()` that calculates
active_power_usage based on target temperature
> 4. Call `recalculate_power()` when target temperature changes or
machine state changes
> 5. Use standard `set_active_power()` and `set_idle_power()` procs
instead of manual registration
> 6. Add `max(0, ...)` safety checks to ensure power never goes negative
> 
> The power calculation should be:
> ```dm
> var/temperature_difference = abs(target_temperature - T20C)
> active_power_usage = max(idle_power_usage, (heat_capacity *
temperature_difference) / 1000)
> ```
> 
> Ensure the fix is applied to:
> - The main `process_atmos()` method
> - The `ui_act()` method when target temperature changes
> - The `CtrlClick()` method for turning on/off
> - Any other places where thermomachine state changes


</details>

Fix vim startup race: flush font actions before spawn, defer commands

Three fixes for the garbled display when launching vim:

1. Flush pending font-chain actions in OnSizeChanged before computing
   grid dimensions. Previously, SetFontPriorityList queued the font
   rebuild for the next render cycle, but OnSizeChanged fired first
   with stale (default Menlo) metrics, causing vim to spawn at the
   wrong size (e.g. 122x43 instead of 138x41). The immediate resize
   that followed produced a visible flash of mispositioned ~ chars.

2. Defer ReplayPendingCommands until after vim outputs its first
   frame. Previously, queued commands (e.g. 'set mouse=a') were sent
   immediately after forkpty, before vim finished initializing. Vim
   echoed the raw ESC + command text as visible characters ('^[:set
   mouse=a'), corrupting the initial screen state.

3. Queue deferred resize when TryResize is called while spawn is in
   progress, applying it after the PTY connection is established.

Also remove all diagnostic logging added during investigation.

* Set up publishing of custom PostgreSQL image to GitHub Container Registry

## New Workflow: publish-postgres.yml
Automatically builds and publishes custom PostgreSQL image to GHCR:
- Triggered on changes to `infra/images/postgres/Dockerfile`
- Builds from infra/images/postgres/Dockerfile
- Pushes to ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}/postgres

- Uses Docker layer caching for faster builds

Updated api-checks and db-migrations workflows to use published image:
- Old: `pgvector/pgvector:0.8.2-pg18` (external image)
- New: `ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}/postgres:develop` (custom image)

## Benefits
- ✅ Single source of truth for PostgreSQL configuration
- ✅ Consistent environment across CI, staging, and production
- ✅ All required extensions (pgvector, PostGIS, pgRouting) available
- ✅ Easy to update and version control

## How It Works
1. Push changes to infra/images/postgres/Dockerfile
2. GitHub Actions builds and publishes the image
3. CI workflows automatically use the latest published image
4. Production deployments use the same image

## Initial Setup
First run will fail because the image doesn't exist yet. After the publish workflow completes, CI will succeed.

* Implement semantic versioning for PostgreSQL image publishing

## Changes
- Updated publish-postgres.yml to use git tags instead of branch triggers
- Publish workflow now triggered by tags matching `postgres-v*` pattern
- Images tagged with semantic versions (v1.0.0, v1.1.0, etc.)
- Latest tag always points to most recent published version
- CI workflows now use `postgres:latest` for simplicity

## Release Process
To release a new PostgreSQL version:

1. Create a git tag:
   ```bash
   git tag postgres-v1.0.0
   git push origin postgres-v1.0.0
   ```

2. GitHub Actions will:
   - Build the Docker image
   - Push to GHCR with tags: v1.0.0, v1, latest
   - Image available at: ghcr.io/company/postgres:v1.0.0

3. CI workflows automatically use `latest` tag

## Version Format
- Tags: `postgres-v1.0.0`, `postgres-v1.1.0`, etc.
- Images: `ghcr.io/company/postgres:v1.0.0`
- Always has: `ghcr.io/company/postgres:latest`

## Future Migration
When ready to split into separate repo:
1. Create hominem-infra repository
2. Move infra/ directory there
3. Update publishing to use separate repo's GHCR
4. Pin main repo to specific versions (v1.0.0, v1.1.0, etc.)
